added_assumptions.label: ass:calibrated-overlap-richness
added_assumptions.statement: Calibrated overlap-richness. The model parameters satisfy (γ−α)_+ ≤ αγ/(α+1) (equivalently, when γ>α, γ ≤ α(α+1)). Under this restriction the local-richness witness strata used in the converse construction — active block with P_X(B_{h}) ≍ h^{α}, treatment contrast |τ| ≍ h, and weak-arm propensity p_{P_{n,±}}(x) ≍ h^{δ} with δ=(γ−α)_+ — satisfy the overlap-decay bound of Assumption ass:overlap-decay (with u=h^{δ/γ}, v=h^{δ} the required P_X(B_h) ≤ C u^{α} v^{1/γ}=C h^{(α+1)δ/γ} holds), hence those alternatives lie in P_{α,γ}.
added_assumptions.source: This is the regime-defining kernel of the conjectured phase transition, not a generic regularity caveat: it is exactly the parameter boundary the overlap-admissibility of the witness fails at, so it is granted as a labelled §7 assumption and the unconditional full-range claim is preserved as an open §8 Conjecture.

proposed_from.topic: Matching minimax characterization of the offline policy-learning welfare-regret rate under one-sided overlap decay and a Tsybakov margin. Estimand: expected welfare regret E[R(pi_hat) - inf_pi R(pi)] of a learned treatment-assignment policy (a risk functional of P; point-identified under strict overlap, so this is an estimation-RATE problem, not identification). THEOREM TO PROVE, two directions: (T1 achievability) a trimmed/clipped-AIPW-score ERM, with propensity clipping threshold t_n tied to a one-sided polynomial overlap-decay exponent gamma (e(x) -> {0,1} like distance-to-boundary^gamma) and operating under a Tsybakov margin exponent alpha at the decision boundary tau(x)=0, attains E[R(pi_hat) - inf R(pi)] <= C n^{-r(alpha,gamma)} with r(alpha,gamma) in closed form; (T2 converse, THE CORE CONTRIBUTION) a localized fuzzy-hypothesis (Le Cam / many-hypothesis) construction with propensity shrinking pi_lambda ~ h^rho on local cubes, whose LOCAL OVERLAP-MARGIN CALIBRATION LEMMA (the Hellinger/KL-equalizing balance among the regret-separation height, the propensity-decay height, and the local cube geometry) is derived explicitly, giving a minimax lower bound n^{-r(alpha,gamma)} matching T1. The named focal object is this calibration lemma plus the matched exponent r(alpha,gamma); the (alpha,gamma) phase boundary (margin-driven branch vs overlap-limited branch) is READ OFF the closed calibration, not assumed. Differentiation: Athey-Wager Ecta'21 give n^{-1/2} under strict overlap with no margin; Luedtke-Chambaz give the margin-alpha fast rate under strict positivity (load-bearing only via influence-function boundedness); Bibaut et al. 2510.15483 treat ONLINE cumulative bandit regret, upper-bound only, with a constant overlap floor and no minimax converse. This proposal is FIXED offline one-sided overlap decay with a matching lower bound, explicitly distinct from adaptive diminishing-exploration policy-learning minimax results.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest catalogue item is stat_ate_overlap_decay_v1, which studies clipped-AIPW weak-overlap rates for scalar ATE estimation and has a tail-localized Le Cam converse. This proposal changes the causal target to learned-policy welfare regret, adds the sign-error and Tsybakov margin transform, and makes the lower-bound focal object a calibrated primitive finite-cell policy-regret construction. The downgraded stat_sa_cate_pointwise_v1 supplies localized CATE machinery but not an integrated welfare-regret minimax rate.","published_axis":"AtheyWager2021PolicyLearning supplies DR offline policy learning under standard overlap; LuedtkeChambaz2017FasterRates supplies margin-based regret conversion under bounded scores; MaSantAnnaSasakiUra2023DRWeakOverlap supplies weak-overlap trimming logic for scalar treatment effects; AudibertTsybakov2007PluginClassifiers and ZhanRenAtheyZhou2021AdaptivePolicy supply lower-bound templates. None states the fixed offline one-sided overlap plus Tsybakov margin exponent with a matching primitive lower-bound construction over the calibrated alpha-gamma region."}
seed_list: Introduce a matched Stat rate frontier r(alpha,gamma) for offline binary policy welfare regret and prove clipped-AIPW ERM achievability plus a localized fuzzy-hypothesis minimax converse.
Construct the primitive local overlap-margin calibration lemma whose cube mass, weak-arm propensity, contrast height, and Hellinger radius yield the lower-bound exponent r(alpha,gamma).
Prove a target-preserving clipped-AIPW policy-ERM upper bound that decomposes score noise, clipping bias, centered plug-in fluctuation, nuisance drift, and margin conversion.
Derive the frontier where pessimistic offline policy learning and clipped-score ERM have the same fixed-overlap-margin regret rate, and where pessimism is conservative.
Give a balanced-policy-learning analogue of the weak-overlap margin rate and characterize when balancing removes the same inverse-propensity penalty as clipping.
Sharpen the strict-overlap margin policy-learning rate by proving the gamma=0 reduction is the boundary case of a one-sided overlap-decay frontier.
Adapt plug-in classification minimax lower bounds to causal policy regret by replacing homoskedastic label noise with inverse-propensity-inflated score noise.
Use overlap information geometry to prove a nonadaptive policy-regret lower bound from indistinguishability of treated and control covariate laws near the decision boundary.
Lift the accepted weak-overlap ATE minimax construction to integrated policy regret by adding the CATE sign-error and Tsybakov margin transform.
Construct an oracle tuning law t_n=n^{-s(alpha,gamma)} for clipped AIPW policy ERM that preserves the untrimmed welfare target under weak overlap.

seed_details.one_line: Introduce a matched Stat rate frontier r(alpha,gamma) for offline binary policy welfare regret and prove clipped-AIPW ERM achievability plus a localized fuzzy-hypothesis minimax converse.

anchor_paper.author: Athey and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: Doubly robust observational policy learning gives utilitarian-regret guarantees under standard overlap but no weak-overlap margin minimax exponent.

seed_details.anchor_followup: The paper's setup makes overlap the score-control condition; extend the regret question to one-sided polynomial overlap decay at the policy boundary.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Rate-as-kernel M11, not routine extension: the lower-bound calibration is the named focal object and must match the upper bound.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Athey-Wager plus Luedtke-Chambaz because both already control policy regret.
seed_details.rebuttal: Those papers separate doubly robust policy learning and margin conversion under bounded scores; neither calibrates inverse-propensity information loss in a fixed offline minimax converse.

seed_details.one_line: Construct the primitive local overlap-margin calibration lemma whose cube mass, weak-arm propensity, contrast height, and Hellinger radius yield the lower-bound exponent r(alpha,gamma).

anchor_paper.author: Zhan et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Adaptive-data policy learning gives lower bounds under diminishing exploration, not fixed nonadaptive overlap geometry.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Replace sequential diminishing exploration by a primitive iid cube construction with fixed propensity geometry.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/lower-bound pattern is stronger because the witness is a generic local packing, not one hand-tuned law.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from adaptive diminishing-exploration lower bounds because scarce treated observations are the same obstruction.
seed_details.rebuttal: The source of scarcity differs: adaptive propensities are products of a logging process, while the proposed class fixes a static propensity surface tied to margin mass.

seed_details.one_line: Prove a target-preserving clipped-AIPW policy-ERM upper bound that decomposes score noise, clipping bias, centered plug-in fluctuation, nuisance drift, and margin conversion.

anchor_paper.author: Ma et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Weak-overlap DR estimation needs bias correction because trimming stabilizes variance but can change the original target.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Carry target-preserving weak-overlap trimming logic from scalar treatment effects to uniform policy regret.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension is nonmechanical because policy ERM adds uniform comparison and margin-to-regret conversion.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Ma-Sant'Anna-Sasaki-Ura because clipping bias is already decomposed.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their target is scalar treatment-effect estimation; policy ERM requires a sign-error/margin transform and a policy-class empirical process.

seed_details.one_line: Derive the frontier where pessimistic offline policy learning and clipped-score ERM have the same fixed-overlap-margin regret rate, and where pessimism is conservative.

anchor_paper.author: Jin et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Pessimistic policy learning avoids uniform overlap through data-dependent bounds, but does not state a fixed alpha-gamma minimax exponent.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Translate data-dependent pessimism bounds into a fixed polynomial overlap class and compare to clipped-score ERM.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M2-style frontier, but field-tier because the two procedures are close and the exact divergence frontier may be secondary.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just comparing two stabilizers for the same policy value.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed asks for a rate-equivalence frontier in observable overlap-margin primitives, not a qualitative comparison.

seed_details.one_line: Give a balanced-policy-learning analogue of the weak-overlap margin rate and characterize when balancing removes the same inverse-propensity penalty as clipping.

anchor_paper.author: Kallus
anchor_paper.claim: Balanced policy learning stabilizes high-variance inverse-propensity weighting but does not resolve the fixed alpha-gamma minimax rate.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Use balancing as a comparator to isolate which rate terms are specific to clipping.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Could collapse to method transfer; retained only if it gives a derived penalty-removal condition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an implementation variant of balanced policy learning.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed contribution is a rate frontier under one-sided overlap decay, not a new optimizer.

seed_details.one_line: Sharpen the strict-overlap margin policy-learning rate by proving the gamma=0 reduction is the boundary case of a one-sided overlap-decay frontier.

anchor_paper.author: Luedtke and Chambaz
anchor_paper.claim: Margin conditions yield faster policy-regret rates when efficient scores are bounded.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Ask what happens to the margin conversion when the efficient score is no longer bounded because overlap decays.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens overlap, stronger only if the result gives a phase boundary rather than a broader sufficient condition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate by replacing bounded score constants with truncated score constants.
seed_details.rebuttal: The lower bound and phase boundary require a joint calibration of margin mass and propensity height, not only an upper-bound truncation.

seed_details.one_line: Adapt plug-in classification minimax lower bounds to causal policy regret by replacing homoskedastic label noise with inverse-propensity-inflated score noise.

anchor_paper.author: Audibert and Tsybakov
anchor_paper.claim: Classification margin lower bounds give fast-rate templates but have no causal inverse-propensity information loss.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Use the classification packing only after inserting causal score noise through local treatment propensities.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension is stronger if the inverse-propensity channel changes the exponent and phase boundary.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just Tsybakov classification with a relabeled regression function.
seed_details.rebuttal: Causal policy regret observes outcomes through treatment arms, so local information is multiplied by weak-arm propensity.

seed_details.one_line: Use overlap information geometry to prove a nonadaptive policy-regret lower bound from indistinguishability of treated and control covariate laws near the decision boundary.

anchor_paper.author: D'Amour et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Strict overlap imposes information-theoretic restrictions on observational studies but not a policy-regret lower-bound calibration.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Turn overlap-as-likelihood-ratio geometry into a policy-regret indistinguishability lower bound.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Impossibility/lower-bound seed is credible only if stated over a nondegenerate local class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an overlap diagnostics paper, not a policy learning theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: That is exactly the gap: it supplies information geometry but no welfare-regret minimax calibration.

seed_details.one_line: Lift the accepted weak-overlap ATE minimax construction to integrated policy regret by adding the CATE sign-error and Tsybakov margin transform.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith
anchor_paper.claim: Accepted weak-overlap ATE rate proves clipped-AIPW achievability and Le Cam converse but not policy regret.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: one-sided overlap decay strictly changes the achievable ATE rate

seed_details.anchor_followup: Extend scalar weak-overlap minimax logic to a learned policy and a welfare-regret loss.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension S1 to S2 is weaker unless sign-error/margin conversion changes the rate; here that is the named kernel.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the accepted ATE overlap theorem plus the welfare identity.
seed_details.rebuttal: ATE estimation has no policy-class comparison, no decision margin, and no sign-error lower-bound packing.

seed_details.one_line: Construct an oracle tuning law t_n=n^{-s(alpha,gamma)} for clipped AIPW policy ERM that preserves the untrimmed welfare target under weak overlap.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith
anchor_paper.claim: Prior reviewer flagged clipped-AIPW fixed-kappa frontier and centered plug-in terms as load-bearing for weak-overlap rates.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: fixed-kappa rate-frontier claim for the displayed clipped AIPW estimator and matching converse

seed_details.anchor_followup: Make the clipping threshold a policy-regret rate object rather than a scalar ATE tuning constant.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Could be mere tuning unless paired with target-preservation and matching converse; score kept field-tier.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just choosing the bias-variance optimal threshold.
seed_details.rebuttal: The threshold must balance margin mass, policy ERM fluctuation, and propensity-tail clipping, not only scalar bias and variance.

proposed_from.literature_map:
1. Athey and Wager (Econometrica 2021, arXiv:1702.02896) establish the offline observational doubly robust policy-learning and utilitarian-regret template under standard overlap.
2. Kitagawa and Tetenov (Econometrica 2018) anchor empirical welfare maximization and policy-class ERM in econometrics.
3. Luedtke and Chambaz (2017, arXiv:1704.06431) give faster policy-regret rates from margin structure when efficient scores remain controlled.
4. Audibert and Tsybakov (Annals of Statistics 2007, arXiv:0708.2321) provide the classification low-noise minimax lower-bound grammar but no inverse-propensity channel.
5. Ma, Sant'Anna, Sasaki, and Ura (2023, arXiv:2304.08974) make trimming bias and target preservation explicit for weak-overlap DR estimators.
6. Kallus (2017, arXiv:1705.07384) gives balance-based policy learning as a stabilization comparator rather than a weak-overlap minimax rate.
7. Jin, Ren, Yang, and Wang (2022, arXiv:2212.09900) analyze pessimistic offline policy learning without uniform overlap through data-dependent upper bounds.
8. Zhan, Ren, Athey, and Zhou (2021, arXiv:2105.02344) give adaptive-data lower bounds under diminishing exploration, distinct from fixed offline overlap geometry.
9. D'Amour, Ding, Feller, Lei, and Sekhon (Journal of Econometrics 2021, arXiv:1711.02582) motivate overlap as an information-theoretic restriction.
10. No source in the Stage -1.1 map states a matched fixed-offline alpha-gamma welfare-regret minimax exponent.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 2
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The matching lower-bound construction is conditional on the calibrated exponent region (gamma-alpha)_+ <= alpha gamma/(alpha+1).
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The minimax lower-bound exponent is claimed only over P_{alpha,gamma}^{cal}; gamma > alpha(alpha+1) remains outside the matched converse claim.
